weather_report:
Late morning in Swanbourne arrives under a patchwork sky, the sun slipping in and out of broken clouds like a shy performer behind a curtain. Light feels gentle rather than fierce, and the air is clear enough to make edges crisp when the sun appears, softer when it doesn’t. There’s no great threat in the clouds today, just a shifting canopy and the sense of a day taking its time.

Expect this pattern to carry on through the afternoon: intervals of bright, pleasant sunshine tempered by cloud. UV will be moderate when the sun breaks through and milder under cover, so sunglasses and a light dab of sunscreen are sensible around lunch. Dress in easy layers—a breathable shirt with a light jacket you can pocket. It’s good weather for modest ambitions: a walk to the shops, a slow loop through the nearest park, pottering with houseplants by an open window, or a quiet errand on foot while the light comes and goes.
